records.ety: [AS. druncen, prop., that has drunk, p. p. of drincan, taken as active. See Drink, v. i., and cf. Drunk.]

defs: Overcome by strong drink; intoxicated by, or as by, spirituous liquor; inebriated.
quotes: Drunken men imagine everything turneth round.

defs: Saturated with liquid or moisture; drenched.
quotes: Let the earth be drunken with our blood.

defs: Pertaining to, or proceeding from, intoxication.
quotes: The drunken quarrels of a rake.
